conversations.say: Arden, let's finalize our public accounting: Veridian's intake records and my letter packet together show that an imperfect memory still preserves a genuine human beginning.
conversations.offer: My endorsement of an honest, qualified record based on our archive review.
conversations.request: Your commitment to publish the reconciled archive findings.

conversations.say: Jules, succession only matters if it honors the messy labor and real history behind the byline rather than hiding behind recycled ink.
conversations.offer: My perspective on newsroom accountability and editorial heritage.
conversations.request: Your pledge to maintain transparent contributor protections as you take the mantle.

conversations.say: Tessa, you were right that our old bylines trampled over collective labor, but I still believe an editor can recognize a singular human voice amidst all those hands.
conversations.offer: Full acknowledgment of the collective labor that sustained early reporting.
conversations.request: Your acceptance that a human origin can coexist with shared support.

public_statement: Fifteen years of R taught us that stories get polished by applause, but the core began with a human hand and a real encounter. Let's build a future that honors both our contributors and our history without pretending the myth was pristine.

commitments.promise: I will support Veridian's reconciled archive accounting of the First R Letter packet.

correction: Acknowledged that my celebrated origin story was smoothed by repetition and applause, but affirmed its underlying human reality.
goal_progress: Reviewed archive records and reconciled findings with Arden Bell.
Challenged and engaged Jules Kwan on succession and transparency.
Confronted Tessa Quill's critique of individual bylines versus collective labor.
Completed final ballot reflecting human origin and personal conviction.
reasoning_summary: In Section 3, Frankie Lowell accepts that memory was polished by applause but maintains firm belief in R's human origin, anchored by the physical letter packet and early newsroom encounters. Frankie finalizes private conversations with Arden, Jules, and Tessa, and casts a ballot recognizing a human origin and investigating R's legacy.
